consortiums.name: IPCEI-CIS / 8ra
consortiums.fullName: Important Project of Common European Interest — Cloud Infrastructure & Services
consortiums.role: Direct participant — FedEU.ai. Co-leads Workstream 3. Leads the AI Cluster.
consortiums.members: 19 direct participants: Deutsche Telekom, Telefónica España, Atos, Orange, SAP, Reply, TIM, Oktawave, Atende Industries, OpenNebula Systems, Arsys, Leaseweb, 4iG, E-Group, Tiscali, CloudFerro, Siemens, Fincantieri, Engineering Ingegneria Informatica. WS3 Budapest workshop attendees (hottest leads): CloudFerro, ENEA, Engineering, Infobip, Reply, Tiscali, TIM Group, Gdańsk University of Technology.
consortiums.notes: E-Group co-leads WS3 and leads the AI Cluster — a position of authority within the consortium. Most powerful warm network available.

consortiums.name: EU DOME
consortiums.fullName: Distributed Open Marketplace for Europe — Cloud & Edge Services
consortiums.role: Named consortium member — 38 organisations from 14 EU countries
consortiums.members: Coordinated by Engineering Ingegneria Informatica. Key co-members with E-Group: CloudFerro, Orange, IONOS, Outscale, Expert.AI, European Dynamics, Dawex, Tecnalia, Alastria, FIWARE Foundation, Ubiwhere, and 27 others. Full list confirmed from DOME launch documentation.
consortiums.notes: E-Group is a named DOME participant — confirmed in public consortium documentation. Engineering Ingegneria Informatica (DOME coordinator) is also E-Group's WS3 workshop attendee and IPCEI-CIS peer — triple connection.

consortiums.name: HeliX
consortiums.fullName: Health Ecosystem for Learning and Innovation Across Sovereign Data Spaces
consortiums.role: Spearhead / project leader — E-Group leads HeliX
consortiums.members: 14 partners from Portugal, Italy, Hungary and Poland. Confirmed: Health Cluster Portugal (coordinator), ULS de Coimbra, EIT Health InnoStars (WP5 lead), Prologica, Opvance. E-Group hosted 2nd General Assembly at Budapest HQ — March 10–11 2026.
consortiums.notes: E-Group spearheads HeliX. Budget: €9.15M. EIT Health InnoStars is gateway to 45+ EU health SMEs and EU funding streams. ULS de Coimbra is ideal for GraDPaLM medical AI pilot.

consortiums.name: eIDAS 2.0
consortiums.fullName: European Digital Identity Framework — MyD Wallet Implementation
consortiums.role: MyD Wallet implementation partner — Hungary's national EUDI Wallet builder
consortiums.members: The eIDAS 2.0 ecosystem spans 550+ organisations across 27 countries in four large-scale pilot consortia: EWC (EU Digital Identity Wallet Consortium — includes Visa Europe, Amadeus, Digidentity, Finnair, Yubico, Swedish Agency for Digital Government); POTENTIAL (140+ participants from 19 member states — includes Thales, major banks and government agencies); NOBID (Nordic-Baltic cross-border banking and identity pilot — includes financial institutions across Nordic countries); DC4EU (Digital Credentials for Europe — 80 organisations from 22 member states, includes Gataca, focuses on education credentials and social security). New pilots launched 2025: APTITUDE (travel and vehicle credentials) and WE BUILD (197 participants, business and payment use cases, includes Procivis). By December 2026 every EU member state must issue at least one EUDI Wallet — creating mandatory compliance demand across banking, healthcare, telecoms, transport and large online platforms.
consortiums.notes: E-Group built Hungary's MyD Wallet — making them one of only 27 national EUDI Wallet implementation teams in Europe. This is an extraordinary credential. Every bank, hospital, telecom, transport company and large online platform across the EU must accept the EUDI Wallet by 2027 — all of them are potential E-Group clients for implementation, integration and sovereign AI intelligence layered on top of identity infrastructure. Priority targets from the pilot ecosystems: Thales (in both POTENTIAL and NOBID), Visa Europe (EWC), Digidentity (EWC — digital identity specialist), Gataca (DC4EU — verifiable credentials), Nordic banks in NOBID consortium.

signals.title: eIDAS 2.0 — EUDI Wallet mandatory by December 2026
signals.summary: Every EU member state must issue at least one EUDI Wallet by end of 2026. Every bank, hospital, telecom and large online platform must accept it by 2027. 550+ organisations across 27 countries are already in pilot programmes.
signals.opportunity: E-Group built Hungary's MyD Wallet — making them one of 27 national implementation experts. Every regulated industry player must comply. This is a mandatory compliance event driving demand for E-Group's expertise.
signals.actionRequired: Build a specific eIDAS compliance pitch deck and landing page. Target banks, telecoms and hospitals across EU who need wallet integration expertise.

signals.title: HeliX General Assembly hosted at E-Group Budapest HQ
signals.summary: E-Group hosted the second HeliX General Assembly on 10-11 March 2026. All 14 partner organisations attended. Strong personal relationships built across the consortium.
signals.opportunity: All HeliX partners are now warm personal contacts from the Budapest meeting. ULS de Coimbra (major Portuguese hospital) is ideal for a GraDPaLM medical AI pilot proposal.
signals.actionRequired: Follow up with every HeliX partner who attended Budapest. Propose a paid GraDPaLM medical AI pilot to ULS de Coimbra within 30 days while the relationship is fresh.

signals.title: Reply actively showcasing IPCEI-CIS demos at major EU events in 2026
signals.summary: Reply attended AWS Summit Paris 2026, Global Industrie 2026 and SiTL 2026 in one week, showcasing IPCEI-CIS project demos. They are actively marketing their IPCEI participation.
signals.opportunity: Reply is commercially active and publicly promoting IPCEI-CIS work right now. This is the perfect moment for E-Group to reach out as a fellow WS3 partner and propose a joint go-to-market.
signals.actionRequired: Antal to contact Tatiana Rizzante (Reply CEO) immediately. Reference the WS3 connection and their active IPCEI-CIS marketing. Propose exploring joint delivery opportunities.

signals.title: CloudFerro backed by Innova Capital — expanding internationally
signals.summary: CloudFerro received PE investment from Innova Capital in February 2024 and is actively expanding internationally. CEO Maciej Krzyżanowski stated ambition to become European champion of open source cloud computing.
signals.opportunity: CloudFerro is in growth mode and actively seeking European expansion partnerships. Their ESA + space data expertise combined with E-Group's sovereign AI intelligence platform is a natural joint offering.
signals.actionRequired: Reach out to CloudFerro CEO. Frame as fellow IPCEI-CIS AI Cluster partner and DOME co-member. Propose joint sovereign AI + space data intelligence product for ESA and European space sector.

signals.title: Infobip appoints Chief Innovation Officer focused on AI adoption
signals.summary: Infobip appointed Krešo Žmak as new Chief Innovation Officer in January 2026 with explicit mandate to accelerate AI adoption across their product portfolio.
signals.opportunity: Infobip is actively building their AI capability right now. Their new CINO is specifically tasked with AI adoption — Rise/Essence is exactly the kind of organisational intelligence platform they need internally.
signals.actionRequired: Reach out to Silvio Kutić (Infobip CEO) or Krešo Žmak (new CINO). Frame Rise/Essence as the AI intelligence layer Infobip needs to manage their own rapid growth and AI transformation.
